headline: Qualcomm’s Reported Tenstorrent Talks Test Lofty Valuation And AI Ambitions
summary: Qualcomm, ticker NasdaqGS:QCOM, is reportedly in talks to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent for US$8b to US$10b. The potential deal would extend Qualcomm's reach into dedicated AI accelerators and data center hardware. This development has not been covered in recent Simply Wall St articles on NasdaqGS:QCOM. NasdaqGS:QCOM is widely known for mobile processors and connectivity chips, and more recently for integrating AI features on devices. The reported Tenstorrent talks point to a wider...

headline: Trending: Robinhood cuts 10% of workforce, Yum Brands sells Pizza Hut, Qualcomm's AI deal
summary: Yahoo Finance's Julie Hyman takes a closer look at some of Tuesday's trending tickers and stories, including Yum Brands' (YUM) sale of Pizza Hut, Qualcomm's (QCOM) reported interest in acquiring an AI chip company, Robinhood (HOOD) cutting 10% of its workforce, and Rackspace's (RXT) job cuts.

headline: XR and AI Leap into the Future at Augmented World Expo USA 2026 as Expo Hall Opens in Long Beach, CA
summary: Today, the Expo Hall opens at Augmented World Expo USA 2026 (AWE), the longest-running and largest event dedicated to Augmented Reality, Extended Reality, Virtual Reality, robotics and AI. Over 5,000 attendees, 250 exhibitors, and 400 speakers are gathering to celebrate the 17th anniversary of AWE and share a landmark year of innovation across XR, spatial computing, AI, and robotics.

content.title: XR and AI Leap into the Future at Augmented World Expo USA 2026 as Expo Hall Opens in Long Beach, CA

headline: Microsoft reveals new Surface laptops starting at $1,499
summary: Microsoft (MSFT) is unveiling its latest generation of Surface and Surface Pro laptops, with price tags starting at $1,499. Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley joins the Market Domination team to dive into the specs of these new PCs, which come amid the lengthy shortage in memory chip supply.

headline: Qualcomm CEO plans to kill app store with AI
summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) CEO Cristiano Amon told CNBC this week that AI agents will displace apps as the primary way consumers interact with their devices. That is not a modest claim. It puts Qualcomm in direct opposition to the app store model that has defined mobile computing for nearly two decades. Amon ...

headline: Bernstein weighs in on potential Qualcomm-Tenstorrent deal
summary: Bernstein maintained its Market-Perform rating on Qualcomm following the report, making no changes to its financial model. Rasgon wrote: "We make no changes to our model at this time, and await any further newsflow that might be forthcoming into the company's analyst day in a couple of weeks. We rate Qualcomm Market-Perform."

content.title: Bernstein weighs in on potential Qualcomm-Tenstorrent deal

We rate Qualcomm Market-Perform."
